ACRONYM TABLE 
Acronym Description 
EAN Estimated Accuracy Number 
EASE Equal-Area Scalable Earth 
FYI First-year ice 
MYI Multi-year ice 
NRT Near Real Time 
OSI SAF Ocean and Sea Ice Satellite Application Facilities 
SIAge Sea Ice Age 
SIC Sea ice concentration 
SID Sea Ice drift 
TAC Copernicus Marine Service Thematic Assembly Centre 
 
KEY DEFINITIONS 
Sea Ice Age 
Intuitively, age is a measure of time, and, therefore, the sea ice age is the time since the beginning of ice 
freezing. However, it makes sense to consider age as a measure of time only when discussing individual 
floes of sea ice. A finite volume, which is represented by a pixel on satellite observations or an element 
in a model grid, always contains a mixture of floes with different ages. The proportion of floes with 
varying ages in this mixture evolves with  time as ice freezes  laterally, drifts and mixes with other ice, 
converges or diverges and refreezes from opened water  in the leads and polynyas . Therefore, 
characterisation of the age of sea ice in a pixel (or model element) with one number is not sufficiently 
informative. 
To overcome this problem, we provide a distribution of sea ice age fractions (see below) and a weighted 
average sea ice age in every pixel. Hereafter, this distribution is referred to as the “Sea Ice Age”. 
Ice age fraction 
Fraction of ice in a pixel with a given age category. The fraction is provided as a percentage of the total 
concentration. The age categories are provided with a precision of one year, counting from the start of 
the freezing season. Example age categories: 
First-year ice (1YI): ice formed during the ongoing freezing season that has not yet experienced melting. 
Second-year ice (2YI): ice formed during the previous freezing season and that has survived one melting 
season. 
Example sea ice age information in a pixel of the Arctic Sea Ice Age product: 
Category 1YI 2YI 3YI 4YI 5YI 6YI 7YI 
Percentage 10% 25% 25% 20% 15% 10% 5%

                                                      Page 4/ 14 
Average sea ice age in the dataset 
The average sea ice age is computed as a weighted average of age of ice fractions with weight equal ti 
the concentration of the ice fraction (see Equation 6). 
Maximum sea ice age in the dataset 
The maximum age is computed as the age of the fraction of ice covering a percentage of the total 
concentration within a pixel above 15%. 
Age derived from the drifting buoy 
The age derived from a drifting buoy equals the time since the buoy was deployed on sea ice. If the buoy 
ended up in open water during the melting season, the age of the buoy is computed as time since 
refreeze of the ice around the buoy 
Accuracy 
The accuracy of the product is computed as the ratio of correct age predictions (i.e., age from the 
product ≤ age from the buoy) to the total number of matchups. 
Matchups 
Collocated in space and time observations by the sea ice age dataset and the drifting buoys. 
First-year ice 
Ice formed during the ongoing freezing season that has not yet experienced melting. 
Second-year ice  
Ice formed during the previous freezing season that has survived one melting season. 
Triangular mesh 
A 2D triangular mesh is a collection of triangles connecting vertices (points) to discretize and represent 
a 2D surface or shape, commonly used in computer graphics and finite element analysis (FEA).
